data.description:
## Problem

rebar has several independent read-only diagnostic surfaces today — `validate` (repo-wide tracker health), `fsck` (event/store integrity), `bridge-fsck` (Jira bridge mapping audit), and the local-dev-env docs manual checks (stale global build, missing pre-commit hook, unset commit-msg hook, mixed-build reducer cache staleness). Each catches a different failure class, but there is no single entry point that runs them all, explains what is wrong in plain language, and — where safe — fixes it automatically. A user hitting a symptom (a claim silently not gated, a stale cache serving old state, a missing venv) has to already know which of these tools to reach for.

## Scope

Add a `rebar doctor` command that aggregates diagnostics across four areas and offers automatic repair where it is safe to do so:

1. **Configuration** — detect config keys unrecognized by the running build (the "unknown key ignored" symptom), missing/misconfigured `rebar.toml` values, conflicting gate settings (e.g. both `require_signature_for_close` and `require_completion_verification_for_close` enabled).
2. **Environment** — detect a global/stale `rebar` shadowing the repo checkouts venv build, a missing or unwired pre-commit hook (`make hooks` not run), a missing `commit-msg` hook in a fresh worktree, missing `[agents]` extra when LLM ops are configured on, missing `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`/`JIRA_API_TOKEN` when their features are enabled.
3. **Ticket data** — wrap/extend `validate` and `fsck` findings (orphaned tickets, cycles, unknown event types from a newer store, stale reducer caches from a mixed-build checkout, malformed attestations) into one report.
4. **Sync artifacts** — wrap `bridge-fsck` (orphaned/duplicate/stale Jira bindings) and surface `PUSH_PENDING` (local `tickets` branch ahead of `sync.remote`) and any bridge probe failures.

For each finding, `doctor` should report: what is wrong, why it matters, and whether it can auto-repair or only advise. Auto-repair should be opt-in per run (e.g. `--fix`) and restricted to unambiguous, reversible actions (e.g. re-running `make hooks`, clearing a stale reducer cache, pushing a `PUSH_PENDING` branch) — never destructive or ambiguous ones (never silently rewrite ticket events, resolve bridge duplicates, or change `rebar.toml` values without confirmation).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `rebar doctor` runs and reports on configuration, environment, ticket data, and sync artifacts in one pass
- [ ] Findings are categorized by severity and each includes a plain-language explanation and remediation hint
- [ ] `rebar doctor --fix` applies only safe, reversible auto-repairs and reports what it changed
- [ ] `rebar doctor --output json` produces a machine-readable report suitable for CI or scripting
- [ ] Existing diagnostic surfaces (`validate`, `fsck`, `bridge-fsck`) continue to work standalone; `doctor` composes them rather than replacing them
- [ ] Documented in the appropriate docs (README/local-dev-env.md) with examples of common findings and fixes
data.title: rebar doctor command
